hero.briefTitle: Practical builds first. Creative range second.

work.title: Projects grouped by category, linked to the real builds.
work.summary: Each card opens its showcase page. The showcase page makes the external GitHub or itch.io link the main action, then adds local screenshots when available.

design.title: A gallery of visual work.
design.summary: This gallery is generated from the images in src/assets/GD. Add more designs to that folder and they will appear here automatically.

skills.title: Tools I can connect to visible work.
skills.summary: The skill list is grouped by how the portfolio is meant to grow: source code, app platforms, game systems, and visual production.

about.title: Software engineering is the main track. Creative work sharpens the edges.
about.summary: The structure below makes the hierarchy clear: software engineering first, then the smaller creative lanes that support how I think, prototype, communicate, and ship.

timeline.title: Education, events, and leadership threads.
timeline.summary: A compact timeline built from your resume, with the public-facing details that matter most for a portfolio audience.

projectPage.notFoundTitle: This showcase does not exist yet.
projectPage.notFoundBody: Use the work section to open one of the prepared project pages.

contact.title: Available for software engineering projects, collaborations, and portfolio reviews.
contact.body: I am strongest when the work combines practical software, clear user flows, and enough creative space to make the final experience memorable.
